statement.p: Sayid scored \frac{21}{25} on a recent exam. Build up this fraction so that the denominator is 100\text{,} so that Sayid can understand what percent score he earned.

solution.p: To change the denominator from 25 to 100\text{,} it needs to be multiplied by 4\text{.} And then we are obligated to also multiply the nuemrator by 4\text{.} So we have:
And the fraction \frac{21}{25} is equivalent to \frac{84}{100}\text{.} (This means Sayid scored an 84\%\text{.})
